text:
To determine how many times Sandra needs to babysit in order to have enough eggs to make 15 Spanish flans, we first need to calculate the total number of eggs required.

Each Spanish flan requires 3 eggs, so for 15 flans, the total number of eggs needed is:
\[ 15 \text{ flans} \times 3 \text{ eggs/flan} = 45 \text{ eggs} \]

Sandra's neighbor gives her a basket of 9 eggs every time she babysits. To find out how many times Sandra needs to babysit to get 45 eggs, we divide the total number of eggs needed by the number of eggs received per babysitting session:
\[ 45 \text{ eggs} \div 9 \text{ eggs/babysitting session} = 5 \text{ babysitting sessions} \]

Therefore, Sandra needs to babysit 5 times to have enough eggs to make 15 Spanish flans.
